claim: The OR-branch at render.ts:130 short-circuits the numeric uncertainty check when passes_threshold===true, allowing a record with uncertainty 0.99 to pass the filter and render mandate wording.

passes_threshold is typed as optional boolean in AdvisorRecommendation with no TypeScript enforcement that it encodes dual threshold; it is copied through from untrusted subprocess JSON without further validation.

Renderer-side guard (Option 1) is the correct shape: extract uncertaintyOk once and apply it to both the passes_threshold and numeric branches at render.ts:129-138, costing ~8 LOC with the confidenceOnly escape hatch preserved at render.ts:133-137.

Producer-contract invariant only (Option 2) is unsafe: the scorer runs as a subprocess whose JSON output is accepted without schema enforcement beyond basic type checks, so no TypeScript test can guarantee passes_threshold encodes dual threshold across scorer versions.

Three test fixtures cover REQ-007/REQ-009: (1) confidence 0.95, uncertainty 0.99, passes_threshold true -> null; (2) confidence 0.80, uncertainty 0.35 boundary pass; (3) confidence 0.81, uncertainty 0.36, passes_threshold true -> null.

Existing livePassingSkill.json fixture (advisor-renderer.vitest.ts:22-24) pins exact brief string 'Advisor: live; use sk-code 0.91/0.23 pass.' which must be intentionally migrated after mandate wording change per REQ-008.
